claims.evidence_text: Although the lower growth limit for wheat is about 42 F, Bauer found better correlation in the GDD predictions by defining the lower threshold temperature as 32 F (0 C).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat GDD base temperature defined as 0 C (32 F) for growth-stage prediction; actual lower growth limit ~42 F (5.6 C).

claims.evidence_text: the lower growth limit for wheat is about 42 F
claims.claim_summary: Lower growth limit for wheat is about 42 F (~5.6 C).

claims.evidence_text: After Haun stage 2.0; If daily Max Temp > 95 F (35 C ) it's set equal to 95 F (35 C).
claims.claim_summary: Maximum temperature cap for wheat GDD after Haun stage 2.0 set at 95 F (35 C); high temperatures retard growth.

claims.evidence_text: When planted at 1.5 inches depth in a bare (no surface residue) moist soil, it takes an accumulation of about 180 GDD (F) beginning with the day after planting until emergence occurs.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat requires ~180 GDD (F base 32) from planting to emergence (Haun stage 0.5) at 1.5 inch depth in moist bare soil.

claims.evidence_text: Following emergence (stage 0.5) standard 8 leaf wheat varieties require the accumulation of 143 GDD (F) for each growth stage through stage 12.0.
claims.claim_summary: Standard 8-leaf wheat requires 143 GDD (F) per Haun growth stage after emergence; phyllochron-equivalent leaf appearance interval.

claims.evidence_text: When planted at 1.5 inches depth in a bare (no surface residue) moist soil, it takes an accumulation of about 180 GDD (F) ... until emergence occurs.
claims.claim_summary: Reference wheat planting depth of 1.5 inches used for emergence GDD calibration.

claims.evidence_text: For the same planting depth and under the same climatic conditions, emergence in seedbeds with surface residue will be delayed by 1 to 2 days.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat emergence delayed 1-2 days in seedbeds with surface residue vs bare soil.
